Title: Network Detective Scan VAF- April-2026
Notes:
Verified and reviewed the VAF scan, which had been stuck in a running state for several days. Upon investigation, confirmed that the connector status was showing offline. Logged into the AD server and verified that no connector was present, so proceeded to download, install, and configure a new connector.

Completed the application setup in Azure, ensuring that all required configurations and integrations were properly in place. After successful setup, initiated the scan and monitored its progress.

Title: Security Audit Report – Via Auto Finance (VAF)
Notes: Reviewed and consolidated multiple Network Detective reports, including network, security, cloud, and consolidated risk assessments. Analyzed external vulnerability scan and limited penetration testing results for the public IP range, identifying key risks such as an outdated OpenSSH version and exposed SSH service. Evaluated Microsoft 365 Secure Score and identified several security control gaps. Compiled all findings into a structured Security Audit Report, including executive summary, risk matrix, and remediation plan. Validated the report for accuracy and audit readiness, created a client-ready document for Via Auto Finance (VAF), and prepared and sent a summary email along with the final report to the client.

Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - VAF-DC-TSVDI-GI: (10.100.103.65) - Disk Utilization is 95%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
Notes: As per the alert connected to  VAF-DC-TSVDI-GI, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 35% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

Title: Spam emails
Notes:
created the rule from powershell- 

# 1. Connect (if your session timed out)
Connect-ExchangeOnline

# 2. The Solution: Block ALL external mail claiming to be from your domain
# This is safe because legitimate internal mail is 'InOrganization'
New-TransportRule -Name "Block External Spoofing - viaautofinance.com" `
-FromScope NotInOrganization `
-SenderDomainIs "viaautofinance.com" `
-RejectMessageReasonText "Security Policy: Emails from this domain must originate from internal authorized servers." `
-StopRuleProcessing $true

# 3. Verify it is active
Get-TransportRule "Block External Spoofing - viaautofinance.com" | Select Name, State, Priority
